allies.rationale_text: Scaffolding gives zone-of-development its practical mechanism by shaping the next reachable step instead of demanding mastery in one leap.
allies.source_quote: The scaffolding process is a truly transferable skill applicable in nearly any domain.

allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking is the long-run payoff of zone-of-development because structured learning of parts eventually reveals interrelationships and the big picture.
allies.source_quote: The ZOD model provides the mechanism for acquiring systems thinking.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Zone-of-development conflicts with status-quo-bias when comfort-zone routines replace stretch-learning progression.
structured_tensions.source_quote: zone-of-development vs status-quo-bias: zone-of-development conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when comfort-zone routines replace stretch-learning progression.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Zone-of-development conflicts with authority-bias when rank-based assumptions about instruction block the learner from getting the right growth challenge.
structured_tensions.source_quote: zone-of-development vs authority-bias: zone-of-development conflicts with authority-bias (authority bias) when learner growth is constrained by rank-based instruction assumptions.

curation_notes.summary: The source is strongest on scaffolding-backed stretch calibration and an explicit tension block around comfort and rank constraints.
donor_drops: Did not preserve deeper deep-work or multidisciplinary relation sprawl beyond the cleanest first-order links.
open_questions: Feynman-technique is source-real as an ally, but the first-order surface is cleaner around scaffolding and systems-thinking.
